fundamentals.name: Roundhill Memory ETF
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: The fund will generally seek to invest primarily in the equity securities of “Memory Companies,” but may also seek exposure to Memory Companies through derivative instruments, such as swap agreements and forward contracts. Under normal circumstances, it invests at least 80% of its net assets (plus borrowings for investment purposes) in equity securities or instruments (i.e., swap agreements or forward contracts) that provide exposure to Memory Companies. The fund is non-diversified.

news.paragraph: Memory chip stocks, including Micron and Sandisk, have seen significant rallies, with Micron specifically jumping 12% after UBS raised its price target to $1,625, citing potential as an AI giant. This surge is driven by strong AI-driven demand and Micron's expansion of U.S. memory production with a Virginia DRAM launch. The positive momentum has also benefited ETFs focused on memory chips, indicating broad sector strength.

headlines.title: Scorching runs for memory chip stocks like Micron and Sandisk have sent this new ETF soaring
Micron stock leads chips higher as UBS sees company becoming a $1.8 trillion AI giant
Micron Jumps 12% After Price Target Raise From $535 to $1,625: Here’s What’s Driving Wall Street’s Most Bullish Target
Lam Research Corporation (LRCX) Rallied on AI-Driven Strong Demand
Micron Expands U.S. Memory Production With Virginia DRAM Launch

examples.body: $DRAM WOWWWWW! And wait til $MU and $SNDK split!!!!!
